created_at: 2026-04-21T08:37:00+08:00
author: Alex Zhang
status: active
guide_effective_from: 2026-04-21
---

# Project Orion — New Team Member Onboarding Guide

**Welcome to the team!** This guide will help you get up and running with Project Orion's development environment and workflows.

**Guide Effective From:** 2026-04-21  
**Last Updated:** 2026-04-21

---

## Environment Setup

### 1. Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure you have:
- macOS 13+ or Ubuntu 22.04+ (Windows users: use WSL2)
- Admin access to your machine
- GitHub account with 2FA enabled
- Slack account (invite sent separately)

### 2. Development Tools Installation

```bash
# Install Homebrew (macOS)
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install required tools
brew install git python@3.11 node@18 docker docker-compose

# Install Python packages
pip install poetry pre-commit black isort flake8 pytest

# Install Node packages
npm install -g yarn @types/node typescript
```

### 3. Repository Setup

```bash
# Clone the main repository
git clone git@github.com:orion-tech/orion-platform.git
cd orion-platform

# Install Python dependencies
poetry install

# Install Node dependencies
yarn install

# Set up pre-commit hooks
pre-commit install
```

### 4. Environment Configuration

```bash
# Copy environment template
cp .env.example .env

# Edit .env with your credentials (ask your buddy for values)
# Required: DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, AWS credentials

# Run database migrations
poetry run alembic upgrade head

# Seed local database
poetry run python scripts/seed_dev_data.py
```

### 5. Verify Setup

```bash
# Start services
docker-compose up -d

# Run backend
poetry run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Run frontend (in another terminal)
cd frontend && yarn dev

# Run tests
poetry run pytest
```

Access the app at http://localhost:3000

---

## Key Tools

### Development

| Tool | Purpose | Documentation |
|------|---------|---------------|
| **FastAPI** | Python web framework | https://fastapi.tiangolo.com |
| **React 18** | Frontend framework | https://react.dev |
| **PostgreSQL** | Primary database | Internal wiki |
| **Redis** | Caching & queues | Internal wiki |
| **Docker** | Containerization | https://docs.docker.com |

### Collaboration

| Tool | Purpose | Access |
|------|---------|--------|
| **GitHub** | Code repository | Auto-granted on join |
| **Slack** | Team communication | Invite sent by HR |
| **Notion** | Documentation | Request access from manager |
| **Figma** | Design files | Request access from design team |
| **Datadog** | Monitoring & logs | Request access from DevOps |
| **LaunchDarkly** | Feature flags | Request access from backend lead |

### Productivity

| Tool | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| **Linear** | Issue tracking | Use for all task management |
| **GitHub Actions** | CI/CD | Configured in repo |
| **AWS Console** | Cloud infrastructure | SSO login required |

---

## Workflow Overview

### 1. Sprint Cycle

We follow 2-week sprints:
- **Monday (Week 1)**: Sprint planning, kickoff notes published
- **Daily**: Standup at 10:00 AM (Slack async updates welcome)
- **Friday (Week 2)**: Sprint review and retrospective
- **Continuous**: Code review, testing, deployment

### 2. Branching Strategy

```
main (production)
  └── develop (integration)
        └── feature/JIRA-123-short-desc
        └── bugfix/JIRA-456-fix-issue
        └── hotfix/critical-fix
```

### 3. Code Review Process

1. Create feature branch from `develop`
2. Make changes, commit with descriptive messages
3. Push branch and open Pull Request
4. Request review from 2 team members
5. Address feedback, ensure CI passes
6. Merge using "Squash and Merge"

### 4. Documentation Standards

All new features require:
- API documentation (OpenAPI spec)
- Architecture Decision Record (if significant)
- Updated runbooks (if operational impact)
- Inline code comments for complex logic

### 5. Deployment Process

- **Staging**: Auto-deploy on merge to `develop`
- **Production**: Manual deployment with runbook
- **Feature flags**: Use LaunchDarkly for gradual rollouts

---

## First-Week Checklist

### Day 1 — Orientation

- [ ] Complete HR paperwork and equipment setup
- [ ] Meet your onboarding buddy (assigned by manager)
- [ ] Join Slack channels: `#general`, `#engineering`, `#backend`, `#frontend`
- [ ] Attend team introduction meeting
- [ ] Set up development environment (see Environment Setup above)

### Day 2 — Codebase Exploration

- [ ] Successfully run the application locally
- [ ] Run the test suite and ensure all tests pass
- [ ] Review project structure and key directories
- [ ] Read through `README.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [ ] Explore the codebase: find the API endpoints, frontend components

### Day 3 — First Contribution

- [ ] Pick a "good first issue" from Linear (label: `onboarding`)
- [ ] Create your first feature branch
- [ ] Make a small change (e.g., fix a typo, add a test)
- [ ] Open your first Pull Request
- [ ] Go through the code review process

### Day 4 — Deep Dive

- [ ] Attend sprint ceremonies (if scheduled)
- [ ] Shadow a senior engineer during code review
- [ ] Review recent Architecture Decision Records
- [ ] Set up monitoring tools access (Datadog, etc.)
- [ ] Schedule 1:1s with team members

### Day 5 — Wrap Up

- [ ] Complete your first merged PR
- [ ] Document any issues encountered during setup (update this guide!)
- [ ] Attend team lunch or virtual coffee chat
- [ ] Complete first-week feedback survey
- [ ] Plan Week 2 goals with your manager

### Week 1 Goals

By the end of your first week, you should be able to:
- [ ] Build and run the application locally
- [ ] Understand the project structure and key components
- [ ] Complete the code review cycle (PR → Review → Merge)
- [ ] Identify who to ask for help on different topics
- [ ] Feel comfortable with our development workflow

---

## Key Contacts

| Role | Name | Slack | Responsibilities |
|------|------|-------|------------------|
| Engineering Manager | TBD | @eng-manager | 1:1s, career growth, blockers |
| Backend Lead | Alex Zhang | @alex | Backend architecture, API design |
| Frontend Lead | TBD | @frontend-lead | UI/UX, component library |
| DevOps Lead | TBD | @devops | Infrastructure, deployments |
| Product Manager | TBD | @pm | Requirements, priorities |
| Your Buddy | Assigned | @buddy | Day-to-day questions, context |

---

## Additional Resources

- [Project Overview](./project_overview.md) — High-level project information
- [API Reference](./api_reference.md) — API documentation
- [Sprint 10 Kickoff Notes](./20260421_sprint10_kickoff.md) — Current sprint context
- [Architecture Decision Records](./20260421_adr_export_service.md) — Technical decisions
- Internal Wiki: https://notion.so/orion-tech (requires access)

---

## Questions?

Don't hesitate to ask! Your onboarding buddy and the `#engineering
